title: URL Shortener
description: Design a scalable URL shortening service that converts long URLs into short, shareable links.

toggles.label: Basic click analytics
toggles.description: Track total clicks and last seen

categories.label: Latency
categories.description: How quickly should the system respond to redirect requests?
categories.placeholder: Example: Target p95 redirect latency under 100ms for good user experience...
examples: Sub-second response time for URL lookups
Minimize latency for good user experience

categories.label: Read Throughput
categories.description: How many redirect requests should the system handle per second?
categories.placeholder: Example: Support thousands of redirects per second for viral content...
examples: Support high read volume (thousands of redirects per second)

categories.label: Write Throughput
categories.description: How many URL creation requests should the system handle per second?
categories.placeholder: Example: Support moderate write volume for URL creation...
examples: Lower write volume compared to reads

categories.label: Availability
categories.description: How reliable should the system be? What level of downtime is acceptable?
categories.placeholder: Example: High availability with 99.9% uptime target...

categories.label: Scalability
categories.description: How should the system scale as traffic grows?
categories.placeholder: Example: Horizontal scaling for 10x traffic spikes...
examples: Handle 10x traffic spikes during viral events

categories.label: Consistency
categories.placeholder: Example: Strong consistency for URL creation, eventual for analytics...
examples: Strong consistency for URL creation to prevent duplicates

apiEndpoints.placeholder: Create a short URL from a long URL. Include request body with URL, optional custom alias, and expiration. Response returns the short URL, code, and metadata.

url.description: The original long URL to shorten

customAlias.description: Optional custom alias for the short code

responses.400: Invalid URL or invalid custom alias

apiEndpoints.placeholder: Redirect endpoint that takes a short code and returns a 302 redirect to the original URL. Handle 404 for not found and 410 for expired links.

apiEndpoints.purpose: Get analytics for a shortened URL
apiEndpoints.placeholder: Get click statistics for a short URL. Returns click count, creation time, and last accessed time.
Update an existing short URL's destination, expiration, or active status. Requires authentication.

active.description: Soft disable or enable the link

apiEndpoints.placeholder: Delete a short URL. Requires admin authentication. Returns 204 on success.

apiEndpoints.purpose: Record a click for analytics in a decoupled service
apiEndpoints.placeholder: Internal endpoint to record click events asynchronously. Used by analytics workers.

ipHash.description: Hashed IP for privacy safe uniques

apiEndpoints.placeholder: Health check endpoint for load balancers and orchestrators.
Readiness check that verifies all dependencies (database, cache) are accessible.

questions.question: Where do requests originate from in the current diagram?
hints: Start with a client/Web node so requests have an entry point.

questions.question: What component sits between users and backend services to terminate HTTP, enforce auth, and route traffic?
hints: Clients need an API Gateway to handle routing, auth, and rate limiting.

questions.question: How does traffic flow from the Web client into the API Gateway at the moment?
hints: Connect the Web client to the API Gateway so requests can actually reach your backend.

questions.question: After the API Gateway, which component should execute the URL creation and redirect logic?
hints: A Service layer is required to handle URL creation and redirect logic.

questions.question: How are requests moving from the API Gateway into the Service layer right now?
hints: Wire the API Gateway to your Service so requests continue through the stack.

questions.question: Where will the platform persist the short-to-long URL mappings for durability?
hints: URL mappings need durable storage. Add a primary database (Postgres is fine).

questions.question: How does the Service reach the database for reads and writes in the current layout?
hints: Connect the Service to the database so it can store and fetch URL records.

questions.question: Right now one Service handles both shortening URLs and redirecting clicks. How could adding a separate redirect Service lighten the work on the write Service?
hints: Split read-heavy redirects from write operations so you can scale each path independently.

questions.question: What in-memory cache would reduce database load for hot redirects while keeping latency low?
hints: A Redis/Memcached layer offloads hot redirects from the database and keeps p95 < 100ms.

questions.question: If cache is present, how does the Service reach it before falling back to the database?
hints: Remember to connect the Service to cache first and fall back to the database on a miss.

questions.question: The Service feeding cache still needs a fallback—can it also reach the database when a slug is missing?
hints: The Service that talks to cache also needs a direct path to the database for cache misses.

questions.question: Can you rename the Services so it's obvious which one handles shortening and which one handles redirects?
hints: Naming each Service after its role (e.g., Shorten vs Redirect) makes the diagram clearer.

onboarding.welcome: Let's walk through building a URL shortener together. I'll guide you step by step through defining requirements, designing the architecture, and testing your solution.

steps.functional: Describe the core features of your URL shortener. What should users be able to do?
steps.nonFunctional: Describe performance requirements: latency targets, throughput (requests/sec), and availability goals.
steps.api: Define the HTTP endpoints your service exposes. Describe request/response formats for creating short URLs and redirecting.
steps.highLevelDesign: Drag components to move them, connect them by dragging from edges. Build your architecture visually.
steps.score: Review your complete design and get feedback on your system architecture.

verification.systemPrompt: You are evaluating a URL Shortener system design practice exercise.

criteria.functional: Check for URL creation and redirect functionality, optional features like analytics and custom aliases
criteria.api: Verify POST endpoint for URL creation, GET endpoint for redirect, proper REST conventions
criteria.design: Ensure cache layer for hot paths, service behind gateway, optional analytics queue
